Category: # 1. Fill in your own durations / probabilities for each row.
# 2. Do NOT change the Variable / Risk Event names -- the engine matches on these.

Variable / Risk Event: copy an existing Risk row and change the name/values.

Category: #      stage    = probability applies per stage (e.g. screenout: 3% per stage)
#      well     = probability applies per well independently
#      campaign = single event for the whole campaign (e.g. crew unavailable)
#    Leave blank to use the built-in consequence library defaults.
#    Fill in to override for a specific risk:
#      extra_wireline_runs  = additional wireline trips triggered by this event
#      extra_milling_plugs  = additional plugs to mill (e.g. isolation plug failure)
# 6. Min/ML/Max Days = N/A for rows where value comes from historical data
#    or is a pure probability (e.g. Cement eval offline).
# 7. Historical rows: Min/ML/Max are populated from the historical_wells.csv
#    distribution at runtime -- the values shown here are reference only.
# Random input  : sampled once per iteration (integer range Min-Max)
# Duration      : triangular-sampled per well (Min / Most Likely / Max days)
# Risk          : Bernoulli event; if it fires

Simulation Impact: Defines number of pads

Category: # Scope = stage: probability applies per stage.

#   If last campaign had 180 stages and 5 gun misfires -> p = 5/180 = 0.028

Remedial CT intervention

Repair and retest required; affects whole well not just a stage
